name: Dave Brubeck
abstract: David Warren Brubeck (; December 6, 1920 – December 5, 2012) was an American jazz pianist and composer. Often regarded as a foremost exponent of cool jazz, Brubeck's work is characterized by unusual time signatures and superimposing contrasting rhythms, meters, and tonalities, and combining different styles and genres, such as classical,…

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Dave Brubeck part of a year, and just had such a wonderful time with his quartet. As a matter of fact when I left the Basie band I went right with the Brubeck quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Rebecca Kilgore
about_quotes.evidence: It’s a tune called “The Duke” by Dave Brubeck. And I’ve just always loved it instrumentally.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rebecca Kilgore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: one of the first records he ever loaned me was Dave Brubeck’s “Jazz Goes to College.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: George Avakian is making splices... edit the Dave Brubeck album that you just put out... I wanted to get the best possible performance for posterity... Dave said that George saved my [blank blank dash].
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: It was of course the members of the Modern Jazz Quartet. It was Dave Brubeck. It was Dizzy Gillespie... I was hired to do the history courses and composition and arranging... We had quite a few.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: I’m playing with Brubeck with the Buffalo Symphony... This is Dave Brubeck... 'Well we want you to come and audition for the band if you would, in New York, next week. I need a horn player.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002
